name: Maui Full
description: Maui is a demigod and trickster figure prominent in Polynesian mythology. Born prematurely and cast into the sea by his mother, Taranga, he was rescued and raised by his grandfather, Tama-nui-i-te-ra. Maui is known for his extraordinary feats of strength and cunning, which he used to benefit humanity. He is credited with slowing the sun's passage across the sky, fishing up islands from the ocean depths, discovering the secret of fire, and attempting to conquer death. His powers include shapeshifting, superhuman strength, and the ability to manipulate natural forces. Maui's role is that of a cultural hero, a trickster, and a benefactor of humankind. He is revered for his ingenuity and his daring exploits, which brought light, land, and essential resources to the Polynesian people. His stories are filled with humor and adventure, showcasing his complex character and his enduring legacy. Despite his heroic deeds, Maui's hubris often leads to his downfall, reminding mortals of their limitations. He is a central figure in Polynesian cosmology, embodying both the potential and the perils of challenging the natural order. Maui's actions continue to shape the world and inspire generations.
subtitle: The Polynesian Trickster and Demigod

keyMyths.title: Maui Slows the Sun
keyMyths.description: Maui noticed the days were too short, hindering work. He trapped the sun with ropes and beat it until it agreed to move slower across the sky.

keyMyths.title: Maui Fishes Up Islands
keyMyths.description: Using his magical fishhook, Maui pulled up islands from the depths of the ocean. These islands became the lands inhabited by the Polynesian people.

keyMyths.title: Maui Discovers Fire
keyMyths.description: Maui tricked the mudhens into revealing the secret of fire. He then shared this knowledge with humanity, allowing them to cook and stay warm.

keyMyths.title: Maui Tries to Conquer Death
keyMyths.description: Maui attempted to enter the body of Hine-nui-te-po, the goddess of night, to reverse the process of death for humankind. He failed when a fantail laughed and woke the goddess, who crushed him.

sources.author: Sir George Grey
